free_text: and tables at En=3.4 MeV of differential cr.sect.
Full set of integral cr.sections is given in 21782.
final publ.,differential cr.sect. at 0.6-2.0 MeV and
integral cr.sections at En between 0.6 -3.4 MeV
Differential cross section measurements for 3.4 MeV
Tandem Van de Graaff at the Centre
The proton beam was pulsed and bunched into
Average current  2 micro Amps, incident on a thin
99.8%  Li-7 target evaporated on a 1 mm
tantalum disc. Neutron energy spread 10 keV.
.Cylindrical at 0 deg to proton beam axis at 10.3 cm
from the target. All samples were 1.5 cm diameter
4 10 cm diam by 2.5 cm thick NE213 liquid
scintillators optically coupled to an XP-1040
photomultiplier. Detectors were at 20 deg intervals.
Flight path from sample to detector was 10 meters.
Overall energy resolution of scattered neutrons
was less than 28 keV. Pulse shape discrimination
was used to reject gamma pulses.
477 keV and 431 keV gammas from proton induced
reactions on Li-7. This normalization was
consistent with an auxiliary neutron detector used
in the TOF mode to one percent.
.Yields corrected for anisotropy effects in the
neutron flux and finite size effects in the sample,
effects. Analytical method of Kinney used.
.Data from tables and text of NEANDC(E)-196.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
Ref. on NEANDC(E)-196 update. Ref. 76LOWELL,1330 and
CEA-N-1969,32 deleted - not relevant to this Entry.
only review (data are used in analysis). BIB update.
